title: Generic 5G NR Handover Procedure
description: Generic 5G NR handover flow with RRC, ML1, MAC, network decision, target access, and user-plane resume.

commonEvidence.hoConfirmedBy: 0x1FFB Encapsulated Event Report: EVENT_NR5G_RRC_HO_STARTED_V2; EVENT_NR5G_RRC_HO_SUCCESS; EVENT_NR5G_RRC_NEW_CELL_IND_V2.
commonEvidence.sourceTargetIdentity: 0xB823 NR5G RRC Serving Cell Info: Physical Cell ID, DL Frequency, Cell Id, Selected PLMN MCC/MNC, TAC. 0xB821 pdu_type 2 BCCH-DL-SCH-Message -> c1 -> systemInformationBlockType1: cellIdentity, Physical Cell Id, Frequency.

1.title: 1. Measurement Configuration
1.decide: 0xB821 DL-DCCH RRCReconfiguration contains measConfig. Use it to understand what the UE should measure and which event can trigger a later MeasurementReport.
1.logcode: 0xB821 NR5G RRC OTA Packet. Full field tree is in the Message page.

evidence.title: Config location
evidence.detail: 0xB821 DL-DCCH-Message -> c1 -> rrcReconfiguration contains measConfig. Click 0xB821 to inspect the full field tree in Message detail.

evidence.title: Event trigger lookup
evidence.detail: Event decision comes from reportConfigToAddModList, not from MeasurementReport alone. MeasurementReport usually carries measId; resolve measId -> reportConfigId -> eventId, for example eventA3.

2.title: 2. Search / Measure
2.decide: 0xB821 delivers RRCReconfiguration with measConfig from source gNB to UE RRC. UE RRC then hands the measurement rules to ML1 via 0xB96E; ML1 searches/acquires cells, records radio observations, filters measurement results, evaluates event/TTT, then UE RRC sends MeasurementReport.
2.logcode: See the relationship diagram below. Click each logcode box to open its structure.

3.title: 3. Measurement Report
3.decide: UE reports neighbor quality to network when a configured reporting event, commonly Event A3, is satisfied.
3.logcode: 0xB821 pdu_type 8 UL-DCCH-Message -> c1 -> measurementReport. Fields: measResults, measId, measResultNeighCells, physCellId, rsrp, rsrq, sinr.

evidence.title: RRC MeasurementReport
evidence.detail: pdu_type 8 UL-DCCH-Message -> c1 -> measurementReport.

4.title: 4. Network HO Decision / Preparation
4.logcode: No direct UE logcode. Inferred when Step 5 appears: RRCReconfiguration contains reconfigurationWithSync.

evidence.title: F1AP
evidence.detail: CU-CP <-> DU control interface. Main path for same gNB-CU / different DU handover preparation.

evidence.title: E1AP
evidence.detail: CU-CP <-> CU-UP interface. Updates bearer / user-plane context; not the main HO decision interface.

evidence.title: XnAP
evidence.detail: source gNB <-> target gNB interface. Used for inter-gNB Xn handover when Xn is available.

evidence.title: NGAP / N2
evidence.detail: gNB <-> AMF control interface. Used for N2 handover; UE-side logs alone cannot confirm it.

5.title: 5. RRCReconfiguration w/ Sync
5.decide: Real handover command/config sent to UE.
5.logcode: 0xB821 pdu_type 4 DL-DCCH-Message -> c1 -> rrcReconfiguration handover command. In NR this is represented by reconfigurationWithSync; some reports describe the same role as mobilityControlInfo. Fields: reconfigurationWithSync, mobilityControlInfo, spCellConfigCommon, physCellId, absoluteFrequencySSB, newUE-Identity, rach-ConfigDedicated, masterKeyUpdate, keySetChangeIndicator, nextHopChainingCount.

evidence.title: Handover command
evidence.detail: pdu_type 4 DL-DCCH-Message -> c1 -> rrcReconfiguration. In NR, the handover indicator is reconfigurationWithSync.

evidence.title: Target cell / RA config
evidence.detail: Target PCI/frequency, new identity, and dedicated RACH resources are carried inside the same command.

evidence.title: Cell identity cross-check
evidence.detail: Use serving-cell info and SIB1 to identify source/target cell identity.

5.identity: 0xB823 NR5G RRC Serving Cell Info: Physical Cell ID, DL Frequency, Cell Id, Selected PLMN MCC/MNC, TAC. 0xB821 pdu_type 2 BCCH-DL-SCH-Message -> c1 -> systemInformationBlockType1: cellIdentity, Physical Cell Id, Frequency.

6.title: 6. Apply Target Config
6.decide: ML1 receives and applies the target-cell physical configuration from the handover command.
6.logcode: 0xB952 NR5G ML1 DL Handover is the main evidence. 0xB950 NR5G ML1 DL Common Config and 0xB951 NR5G ML1 DL Dedicated Config may show related common/dedicated target config snippets. Fields: Target Dl Cell Frequency, Target Phy Cell Id, Crnti CFG, CG Add Mod, Cell Info, DL Cell frequency, BWP, CORESET, Search Space, TDD UL DL CFG.

evidence.title: Apply target handover config
evidence.detail: ML1 applies target downlink cell and C-RNTI / carrier configuration.

evidence.title: Related physical config
evidence.detail: Common/dedicated ML1 config snippets that help explain target BWP, CORESET, Search Space, and TDD settings.

evidence.title: Target identity cross-check
evidence.detail: Confirm target PCI/frequency against serving cell info and SIB1.

6.identity: 0xB823 NR5G RRC Serving Cell Info: Physical Cell ID, DL Frequency, Cell Id, Selected PLMN MCC/MNC, TAC. 0xB821 pdu_type 2 BCCH-DL-SCH-Message -> c1 -> systemInformationBlockType1: cellIdentity, Physical Cell Id, Frequency.

7.title: 7. Handover Execution
7.decide: UE starts switching from the source cell to the target cell; this is a cross-layer execution interval between RRC command, ML1 target config application, and MAC target access.
7.logcode: 0xB952 NR5G ML1 DL Handover plus 0xB9A7 NR5G ML1 DLM2 CA Metrics Request. Fields: Event: HANDOVER_START, Event: HANDOVER_END, PCI, DL EARFCN, Band.

evidence.title: Execution timing
evidence.detail: Use ML1 event values to mark the start/end of the handover execution window.

evidence.title: Target context
evidence.detail: Use the target-cell handover config around the same timestamp.

8.title: 8. Random Access
8.decide: UE starts random access on target cell.
8.logcode: 0xB889 NR5G MAC RACH Trigger. Fields: Rach Reason: HANDOVER, CRNTI, RACH Contention, Carrier Id, RA Id. Also 0xB88A NR5G MAC RACH Attempt.

evidence.title: RACH trigger
evidence.detail: MAC starts random access because of handover.

evidence.title: RACH attempt
evidence.detail: Detailed Msg1/Msg2/Msg3/Msg4 attempt evidence, when present.

9.title: 9. Random Access Success
9.decide: Random access on the target cell succeeds; if the explicit success field is absent, infer success from later handover completion.
9.logcode: Best evidence: 0xB88A NR5G MAC RACH Attempt with RACH Result: SUCCESS and, when applicable, Contention Type: CONT_FREE. Supporting evidence: 0xB9BF NR5G ML1 Search HO Acq Confirm. Fields: RACH Result, SUCCESS, Contention Type, CONT_FREE, Result, ARFCN, Cell Id.

evidence.title: MAC RA success
evidence.detail: Best direct evidence when the attempt record exposes the final result.

evidence.title: HO acquisition confirm
evidence.detail: Supporting ML1 evidence that the target-cell acquisition succeeded.

evidence.title: Fallback inference
evidence.detail: If no explicit RA success field appears, infer success from Step 10 and HO success events.

9.confirm: If no explicit RA success field appears, use Step 10 and 0x1FFB events as confirmation: EVENT_NR5G_RRC_HO_SUCCESS; EVENT_NR5G_RRC_NEW_CELL_IND_V2.

10.title: 10. RRCReconfigurationComplete
10.decide: UE confirms handover completion to target.
10.logcode: 0xB821 pdu_type 8 UL-DCCH-Message -> c1 -> rrcReconfigurationComplete. Fields: ul-dcch -> c1 -> rrcReconfigurationComplete, Physical Cell Id, Frequency, rrc-TransactionIdentifier.

evidence.title: RRC completion
evidence.detail: pdu_type 8 UL-DCCH-Message -> c1 -> rrcReconfigurationComplete.

evidence.title: HO event confirmation
evidence.detail: Use event container records as strong timing confirmation.

evidence.title: New serving cell identity
evidence.detail: Cross-check the target identity after completion.

10.confirm: 0x1FFB Encapsulated Event Report: EVENT_NR5G_RRC_HO_STARTED_V2; EVENT_NR5G_RRC_HO_SUCCESS; EVENT_NR5G_RRC_NEW_CELL_IND_V2.
10.identity: 0xB823 NR5G RRC Serving Cell Info: Physical Cell ID, DL Frequency, Cell Id, Selected PLMN MCC/MNC, TAC. 0xB821 pdu_type 2 BCCH-DL-SCH-Message -> c1 -> systemInformationBlockType1: cellIdentity, Physical Cell Id, Frequency.

11.title: 11. Post-HO Operation
11.decide: UE operates on the new serving cell: serving-cell state updates, MIB/SIB decoding starts, and user-plane data resumes.
11.logcode: Post-handover evidence: 0xB825 NR5G RRC Configuration Info, 0xB823 NR5G RRC Serving Cell Info, and 0xB821 broadcast/system-info classes: pdu_type 1 BCCH-BCH-Message -> mib, pdu_type 2 BCCH-DL-SCH-Message -> systemInformationBlockType1, and pdu_type 14/15/16/17 SIB2/SIB3/SIB4/SIB5 when present. If 0xB822 is available, it can also show MIB/SIB decoding. User-plane resume can be inferred from 0xB888 NR5G MAC PDSCH Stats and 0xB881 NR5G MAC UL TB Stats.

evidence.title: New serving-cell state
evidence.detail: Use RRC config/serving-cell records to confirm the UE is operating on the new cell.

evidence.title: Broadcast/system info after HO
evidence.detail: B821 variants show MIB/SIB1/SIB2-SIB5 when decoded after moving to the target cell.

evidence.title: User-plane data resumes
evidence.detail: No single RRC message means data resumed; infer from MAC downlink/uplink counters after HO.

evidence.title: Handover type classification
evidence.detail: After source and target cell identity are known, split NCI into gNB ID and local cell ID. Same gNB ID with different local cell ID means intra-gNB; different gNB ID means inter-gNB. PCI is not sufficient for this classification.
